summary.intent: Build a system to map checkpoints (identified by IDs in git commit messages) to their git commit hashes (OIDs), and persist this mapping to the database when new checkpoints are indexed.
summary.outcome: Created `src/gitUtils.ts` with helpers to extract checkpoint IDs from commit messages and find matching commits across branches; added `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` Prisma table; wired the OID save into `indexCheckpointV2`; consolidated V1/V2 indexer functions and updated all call sites.

repo: Checkpoints are associated with git commits via a 'Entire-Checkpoint: <id>' line in the commit message body.
The project uses a V1/V2 dual-indexer pattern; V2 is the current preferred path and V1 was removed during this session.
Prisma schema is in `prisma/schema.prisma`; migrations are applied with a push command and require a rebuild to regenerate the client.
The repo path available at indexing time is `repoDir`, sourced from server.ts.
handler.ts calls `indexAllCheckpoints` dynamically and consumes its return value for `newMessages`.
gitlog npm library requires the repo path as an absolute path and supports both callback and promise-style APIs.

code.finding: New file: contains `getCheckpointIdFromCommitMessage` (regex extract) and `findCommitForCheckpoint` (searches branch, then main/master fallback, returns {hash, subject, branch} or null).
TypeScript cannot narrow through a boolean alias (`isNew`) assigned from a conditional — must use non-null assertion (`!`) after the if/else block instead.
`CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` uses `gitOid` as primary key with an index on `checkpointId`, since a checkpoint ID can theoretically map to multiple commits.

workflow: When gitlog's API shape was unclear, the assistant ran multiple exploratory Bash commands to test the library before committing to an implementation.
TypeScript narrowing issues through boolean aliases are a common gotcha — prefer direct conditional checks or non-null assertions over intermediate booleans when narrowing is needed downstream.

friction: Extensive trial-and-error (9+ Bash commands) required to determine correct gitlog API usage — callback vs promise, execOptions, ESM vs CJS, absolute vs relative path.
TypeScript failed to build because a `isNew` boolean alias broke type narrowing on `checkpointMeta`, requiring a non-null assertion fix.
Multiple rounds of reading `indexer.ts` were needed to locate exact edit points due to the file's length and the V1/V2 interleaving.
open_items: The `saveGitOidMapping` helper silently skips if the mapping already exists — no backfill mechanism exists for checkpoints indexed before this feature was added.
`repoPath` is optional on `indexCheckpointV2`/`indexAllCheckpointsV2`, so OID mapping is silently skipped when not provided — callers that don't pass it won't get mappings.
No tests were written for `getCheckpointIdFromCommitMessage` or `findCommitForCheckpoint`.
